site_title: 주간 AI ｜ AI 활용 기계공학 논문 브리핑

ai.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
ai.summary: Modern image generation demands a single model that unifies diverse capabilities, including text-to-image (T2I), local editing, and global editing. However, these capabilities are rarely naturally aligned and often conflict. For instance, editing tends to degrade T2I performance, while global and local editing interfere with each other. Consequently, effectively composing these capabilities has become a central challenge for image generation model training. To tackle this, we introduce DanceOPD, an on-policy generative field distillation framework for flow-matching models that routes each sample to one capability field, queries one low-noise student-induced state, and trains with a simple velocity MSE objective. With each capability source defined as a velocity field over the shared flow state space, the student learns from fields queried on its own rollout states to compose expert capabilities. This formulation also absorbs operator-defined fields such as classifier-free guidance. Comprehensive experiments on T2I, editing, realism-field absorption, and CFG absorption show that our approach improves multi-capability composition, strengthening target capabilities while preserving anchor generation quality. We believe this work establishes a practical route for generative field distillation in flow-matching models.
ai.summary_ko:
- 최신 이미지 생성 모델은 텍스트-이미지(T2I), 로컬 편집, 글로벌 편집 등 다양한 기능을 통합해야 합니다.
- 하지만 이러한 기능들은 서로 충돌하여 T2I 성능 저하 및 편집 간섭 문제를 야기합니다.
- DanceOPD는 플로우 매칭 모델을 위한 온-정책 생성 필드 증류 프레임워크를 제안합니다.
- 이 프레임워크는 각 샘플을 특정 기능 필드로 라우팅하고, 학생 모델의 롤아웃 상태에서 전문가 필드를 학습합니다.
- DanceOPD는 다중 기능 구성을 개선하고, 목표 기능을 강화하며, 기존 생성 품질을 유지합니다.

ai.title: Wan-Streamer v0.1: End-to-end Real-time Interactive Foundation Models
ai.summary: We present Wan-Streamer, a native-streaming, end-to-end interactive foundation model designed from the ground up for real-time, low-latency, full-duplex audio-visual interaction. Wan-Streamer seamlessly models language, audio, and video as both input and output within a single Transformer, where the sequence is represented as interleaved visual, audio, and text input tokens together with visual, audio, and text output tokens, coordinated by block-causal attention for incremental streaming. Unlike cascaded interactive systems that rely on separate VAD, ASR, language, TTS, audio-driven animation, or video-generation modules, Wan-Streamer does not rely on external language, speech, avatar, or video-generation modules: perception, reasoning, generation, response timing, turn management, and cross-modal synchronization are learned jointly within one unified model, reducing pipeline latency and error accumulation. To support natural audio-visual responsiveness, we redesign the entire stack around streamability, including causal encoders, causal decoders, block-causal attention, and low-latency multimodal token scheduling, enabling streaming units as short as 160 ms at 25 fps. Wan-Streamer achieves approximately 200 ms model-side response latency and approximately 550 ms total interaction latency when combined with 350 ms bidirectional network latency, supporting sub-second duplex audio-visual communication. These results position Wan-Streamer as a unified, end-to-end, multimodal interactive foundation model for low-latency streaming interaction.
ai.title_ko: Wan-Streamer v0.1: 실시간 상호작용을 위한 종단간(End-to-end) 파운데이션 모델
ai.summary_ko:
- 기존의 다단계 시스템과 달리, Wan-Streamer는 실시간 양방향 오디오-시각 상호작용을 위한 통합 모델입니다.
- 이 모델은 언어, 오디오, 비디오를 단일 트랜스포머(Transformer) 내에서 입력과 출력으로 동시에 처리합니다.
- 인과적 인코더/디코더와 블록-인과적 어텐션(attention)을 활용하여 낮은 지연 시간의 스트리밍을 지원합니다.
- Wan-Streamer는 약 200ms의 모델 응답 지연 시간과 총 550ms의 상호작용 지연 시간을 달성합니다.
- 이는 지각, 추론, 생성, 응답 타이밍 등을 통합 학습하여 파이프라인 지연과 오류를 줄인 혁신적인 접근입니다.

mech.title: On limitations of polyconvexity
mech.summary: Polyconvex constitutive modeling is attractive as it guarantees stability of numerical simulations and can improve the generalization behavior of material models. However, in certain applications, polyconvex formulations perform poorly in reproducing the underlying ground truth material response, which can effectively preclude their practical use. In this work, we address this issue and investigate the limitations of polyconvex constitutive modeling. The main contributions of this paper are as follows: (1) We analyze the theoretical reasons why polyconvexity may, in some cases, impose overly restrictive constraints that limit the achievable accuracy of constitutive models. Thereby, we provide analytical ellipticity guarantees for two non-polyconvex Mooney-Rivlin type potentials. (2) We investigate the practical limitations of polyconvex physics-augmented neural network constitutive models using two representative formulations: models using structural tensor-based invariants and models using signed singular values. Their performance is evaluated on datasets obtained from homogenized microstructured materials, and their predictive capabilities are assessed in finite element simulations. (3) Overall, we provide an overview of benefits, limitations, and mitigation strategies of polyconvex constitutive modeling.
mech.summary_ko:
- 다중볼록성 구성 모델은 수치 시뮬레이션 안정성을 보장하지만, 특정 재료 반응 재현에 한계가 있습니다.
- 본 연구는 다중볼록성 구성 모델링의 이론적, 실용적 한계를 분석합니다.
- 비다중볼록 Mooney-Rivlin 유형 포텐셜에 대한 분석적 타원성 보장을 제공합니다.
- 구조 텐서 기반 불변량 및 부호 있는 특이값을 사용하는 모델의 성능을 평가합니다.
- 다중볼록성 구성 모델링의 장점, 한계 및 완화 전략을 포괄적으로 제시합니다.

mech.title: Multi-parameter optimization of cylinder upsetting using FE and ANN
mech.summary: Cold cylinder upsetting of 16-MnCr5 low-alloy steel is a critical bulk metal forming operation in which energy efficiency and workpiece mechanical integrity are strongly governed by process parameter selection. This study presents a systematic finite element (FE) simulation-based investigation of the combined effects of friction coefficient (μ = 0.05–0.20), upper die velocity (v = 15–30 mm s−1), and workpiece initial temperature (T0 = 25 °C–100°C) _ all within the cold forging regime (T/Tm = 0.172–0.215) _ on four key process outputs: total energy consumption, yield stress, final workpiece temperature, and effective maximum plastic strain rate. A full-factorial design of experiments comprising 64 SIMUFACT Forming FE simulations was employed to generate a comprehensive process response dataset. A feedforward artificial neural network (ANN) surrogate model, implemented in MATLAB and trained on 80% of the dataset (R2 ≈ 0.99), was developed to capture the complex nonlinear relationships between process parameters and outputs, offering a computationally efficient alternative to repeated FE evaluation. Results demonstrate that friction coefficient is the dominant parameter governing energy consumption and yield stress, while initial temperature exerts the greatest influence on final workpiece temperature. The optimal process conditions _μ = 0.05, v = 15 mm s−1, T0 = 100 °C_ are identified as achieving minimum energy consumption within the investigated design space. The proposed FE-ANN framework provides a practical and accurate predictive tool for cold forging process design and optimization.
mech.title_ko: FE 및 ANN을 활용한 실린더 업세팅의 다중 매개변수 최적화
mech.summary_ko:
- 16-MnCr5 저합금강의 냉간 실린더 업세팅은 공정 매개변수 선택이 중요합니다.
- 이 연구는 FE 시뮬레이션으로 마찰 계수, 상부 다이 속도, 초기 온도의 영향을 분석합니다.
- 64개의 SIMUFACT Forming FE 시뮬레이션으로 데이터를 생성하고 ANN 모델을 훈련했습니다.
- 마찰 계수가 에너지 소비와 항복 응력에, 초기 온도가 최종 온도에 가장 큰 영향을 줍니다.
- 이 FE-ANN 프레임워크는 냉간 단조 공정 설계 및 최적화를 위한 예측 도구를 제공합니다.
